title: Daddy's Head
review: Some really awkward line reads, especially from minor roles. The whole movie just feels a bit cheap. There are many great horror movies made on shoestring budgets, where you are swept along without thinking about it. Watching this one, I kept thinking about the ways in which it felt cheap and amateurish. It moves fast without spending time to develop characters. There's a load of jump scares but none of them excited me. If Damian McCarthy or Ari Aster wrote/directed this, imagine how much better it would have been. Watch {Hereditary} or {The Babadook} instead.
spoilers: I liked the wood structure. I liked the dog. The ending was good but I kind of wish the surprise time jump happened a bit earlier, and so there could be more storytelling from older Isaac's perspective. I could have done with a little more information about the daddy head, even if it was in the form of ambiguous clues.
